events.title: US Admiral Claims Iran's Threatening Capability Dramatically Reduced
events.desc: Admiral Brad Cooper, head of U.S. Central Command, stated that U.S. bombings have significantly reduced Iran's ability to threaten its neighbors and U.S. interests, setting back Iran's defense industry by 90%. He emphasized the tactical successes of the military campaign against Iran and reduced the danger posed by the country to the Middle East.

events.title: Iran Urges BRICS Nations to Condemn US-Israeli Aggression
events.desc: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi called on BRICS nations to condemn what he termed "US-Israeli war aggression." He also accused the United Arab Emirates of direct involvement in military operations against Iran.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Southern Lebanon Kill 22, Including Children
events.desc: A series of Israeli air strikes in southern Lebanon resulted in the deaths of 22 people, including eight children, according to the Lebanese health ministry. This incident occurred despite a fragile ceasefire that had been holding between Israel and Hezbollah.

events.title: Lebanon Reports 39 Killed in Israeli Strikes
events.desc: The Lebanese health ministry reported that 39 people were killed in Israeli strikes, with Israel and Hezbollah continuing to exchange fire despite a recent ceasefire announcement. This escalation highlights the ongoing volatility in the region.

events.title: Yemen Government and Houthis Agree to Largest POW Exchange
events.desc: The Yemeni government and the Iran-aligned Houthi group agreed to exchange over 1,600 prisoners, facilitated by the UN. This represents the largest release of detainees since the conflict in Yemen began.

events.title: Iran Allows Transit of Chinese Vessels in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: Iran has reportedly begun allowing some Chinese vessels to transit through the Strait of Hormuz following an agreement on Iranian management protocols for the waterway. This development occurred as U.S. President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping discussed keeping the strait open.

events.title: US-Led Mission to Secure Strait of Hormuz to Include UK Jets, Drones, and Warship
events.desc: The United Kingdom has pledged to contribute fighter jets, drones, and a warship to a multinational mission aimed at defending the Strait of Hormuz. The mission, involving over 40 nations, is set to commence when conditions permit.

events.title: Iran's Offer to End War Deemed 'Unacceptably Weak' by Trump
events.desc: U.S. President Trump described Iran's counteroffer to end the war as "unacceptably weak" and "on massive life support," criticizing the month-long ceasefire proposal.

events.title: EU Agrees Sanctions on Israeli Settlers Over West Bank Violence
events.desc: The European Union has agreed to impose sanctions on Israeli settlers responsible for violence in the West Bank. The EU's foreign policy chief stated that "extremism and violence carry consequences," while Israel denounced the move as "arbitrary."

events.title: Israeli Nationalists March Through East Jerusalem; Tensions Rise
events.desc: Thousands of Israeli nationalists participated in the annual Jerusalem Day march through the Muslim quarter of the Old City, an event that has become a show of force for Jewish nationalists and is viewed by Palestinians as a provocation. Chants of "Death to Arabs" were reported.

events.title: Efforts to 'Make Iran Ungovernable' Target Police Infrastructure
events.desc: Bellingcat identified at least 80 police stations or infrastructure related to law enforcement and the Basij paramilitary group damaged or destroyed in the first three weeks of the US and Israeli conflict against Iran. Experts suggest the aim is to degrade the Iranian regime's "repressive capacity."

events.title: Satellite Imagery Shows Widespread Demolitions in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Satellite imagery reveals that at least 46 out of 54 towns and villages within the Israeli "Yellow Line" in southern Lebanon have sustained heavy damage or complete flattening. Much of this destruction has occurred despite the fragile ceasefire.

events.title: Houthi, Yemeni Government Exchange Over 1,600 POWs
events.desc: The Yemeni government and the Houthi group have agreed to exchange over 1,600 prisoners, in what is described as the largest prisoner swap since the conflict began. The UN special envoy's office confirmed the agreement.

events.title: Hezbollah Support Endures in South Lebanon Amidst Failed Ceasefire
events.desc: Despite ongoing Israeli attacks, many residents in southern Lebanon continue to support Hezbollah, viewing the armed group as their primary defender. This enduring support persists even as the ceasefire remains fragile and Israeli strikes continue.

events.title: Iranian Naval Forces Seize 'Floating Armoury' Vessel
events.desc: Reports indicate that Iranian military personnel have seized a vessel described as a 'floating armoury' in the Gulf of Oman, raising tensions in the strategic waterway.
